[30;1m214 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m215 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m216 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m217 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m218 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m
[30;1m219 | [0m[35mchecked for validity.  Since those two are valid, matching proceeds.[0m
[30;1m220 | [0m[35m[0m

[31m--[ [0m[34mMatch #[0m[33m330[0m[34m of [0m[33m1213[0m[31m ]--[0m

[30;1m213 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m214 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m215 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m216 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m217 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m

[30;1m198 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m199 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m200 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m201 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m202 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m

[30;1m212 | [0m[35mIf brace expansion is not disabled, then it is performed before any[0m
[30;1m213 | [0m[35mother interpretation of the glob pattern.  Thus, a pattern like[0m
[30;1m214 | [0m[35m`+(a|{b),c)}`, which would not be valid in bash or zsh, is expanded[0m
[30;1m215 | [0m[35m**first** into the set of `+(a|b)` and `+(a|c)`, and those patterns are[0m
[30;1m216 | [0m[35mchecked for validity.  Since those two are valid, matching proceeds.[0m

[30;1m199 | [0m[35mThe double-star character `**` is supported by default, unless the[0m
[30;1m200 | [0m[35m`noglobstar` flag is set.  This is supported in the manner of bsdglob[0m
[30;1m201 | [0m[35mand bash 4.1, where `**` only has special significance if it is the only[0m
[30;1m202 | [0m[35mthing in a path part.  That is, `a/**/b` will match `a/x/y/b`, but[0m
[30;1m203 | [0m[35m`a/**b` will not.[0m
[30;1m204 | [0m[35m[0m

[30;1m68 | [0m[35m* `negate` True if the pattern is negated.[0m

[30;1m8 | [0m[35mAsync provides around 20 functions that include the usual 'functional'[0m
[30;1m9 | [0m[35msuspects (map, reduce, filter, forEach…) as well as some common patterns[0m
[30;1m10 | [0m[35mfor asynchronous control flow (parallel, series, waterfall…). All these[0m
[30;1m11 | [0m[35mfunctions assume you follow the node.js convention of providing a single[0m
[30;1m12 | [0m[35mcallback as the last argument of your async function.[0m

[30;1m788 | [0m[35m### parallelLimit(tasks, limit, [callback])[0m
[30;1m789 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m790 | [0m[35mThe same as [`parallel`](#parallel), only `tasks` are executed in parallel [0m
[30;1m791 | [0m[35mwith a maximum of `limit` tasks executing at any time.[0m
[30;1m792 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m793 | [0m[35mNote that the `tasks` are not executed in batches, so there is no guarantee that [0m
[30;1m794 | [0m[35mthe first `limit` tasks will complete before any others are started.[0m
